status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

features.name: Live pose landmark detection
features.description: Load MediaPipe PoseLandmarker and emit landmarks from webcam frames without blocking rendering.
features.user_visible_behavior: After camera permission, the calibration screen receives stable live body landmarks while the UI remains responsive.
verification: Run npm run build successfully.
Open the app in a supported browser and grant camera access.
Confirm landmarks are produced continuously and camera cleanup stops the stream when leaving the screen.
evidence: 2026-07-16: npm run build passed; Vite transformed 30 modules and produced dist/assets/index-CblomtYB.js.
2026-07-16: User granted camera access and confirmed continuous Tracking 33 body landmarks output.
2026-07-16: User confirmed the webcam indicator turns off after closing or leaving the page.
features.notes: Keep pose inference independent from requestAnimationFrame rendering. Implementation loads MediaPipe PoseLandmarker with GPU-to-CPU fallback, runs detection on a separate 24 FPS timer, reports landmark count in calibration UI, and cleans up the stream/model on unmount.

features.name: Per-user jump and squat calibration
features.description: Capture representative jump and squat samples and derive thresholds for the current player.
features.user_visible_behavior: Every run guides the player through framing, one jump, and one squat before gameplay can begin.
verification: Complete framing, jump, and squat calibration in order.
Confirm calibration produces finite jump and squat thresholds from the captured samples.
Confirm gameplay cannot start with missing or invalid thresholds.
evidence: 2026-07-16: npm test passed calibration threshold and incomplete-session tests (2/2).
2026-07-16: npm run build passed after calibration UI and flow implementation; Vite transformed 32 modules.
2026-07-16: User completed framing, jump, and squat in order and observed transition to the currently blank GameScreen placeholder.
2026-07-16: Automated weak-movement test confirms tiny jump/shallow squat samples cannot finalize thresholds or start gameplay.
features.notes: Per-user thresholds are derived from the captured standing, jump, and squat samples; weak or incomplete samples are rejected.

features.name: Buffered jump and squat classification
features.description: Classify jump, squat, or none from a rolling landmark buffer and calibrated thresholds.
features.user_visible_behavior: A calibrated jump emits jump, a held squat emits squat, and neutral/noisy motion emits none.
verification: Verify representative landmark sequences classify jump, squat, and none correctly.
Confirm isolated noisy frames do not emit repeated actions.
Run npm run build successfully.
evidence: 2026-07-16: npm test passed jump, held-squat, and isolated-noise classifier cases; full suite passed 6/6.
2026-07-16: npm run build passed with the classifier implementation; Vite transformed 32 modules.
features.notes: Classification remains pure and independent of DOM and canvas APIs; it consumes calibrated thresholds and a bounded landmark buffer.

features.name: Pose and game split-screen layout
features.description: Build the responsive gameplay shell with a 35 percent pose-tracking panel on the left and a 65 percent game panel on the right.
features.user_visible_behavior: During gameplay, the player sees a mirrored camera feed, landmark overlay, tracking status, and recognized action beside a larger 16:9 game canvas; narrow screens stack the panels without losing readability.
verification: Open the gameplay screen at a laptop-sized landscape viewport and confirm the pose and game panels use an approximately 35/65 horizontal split.
Confirm the left panel shows the mirrored camera, landmark overlay, tracking status, and jump, squat, or ready feedback while the game panel remains responsive.
Resize to a narrow or portrait viewport and confirm the pose panel stacks above the game without stretching or cropping the 16:9 game canvas.
Confirm pose landmark updates remain smooth without causing React to render once per raw landmark frame.
Run npm run build successfully.
evidence: 2026-07-16: npm test passed 6/6 after split-screen integration.
2026-07-16: npm run build passed with the responsive pose/game shell; Vite transformed 33 modules.
features.notes: Follow docs/UI-build-plan.md. Implementation uses an overlay canvas for landmarks, a 35/65 desktop grid, and a stacked narrow-screen media query. Desktop and narrow-screen visual behavior still require browser observation before passing.

features.name: Playable runner loop
features.description: Connect classified actions to game state, obstacle spawning, collisions, rendering, score, and miss tolerance.
features.user_visible_behavior: Jump clears low obstacles, squat clears high obstacles, misses reduce the buffer, and repeated misses allow the monster to catch the player.
verification: Start a calibrated run and confirm obstacles and score advance continuously.
Confirm jump and squat affect the correct obstacle types.
Confirm one miss does not end the game and repeated misses reach game over.
features.notes: Preserve pose -> action -> state -> render data flow.

features.name: MVP lifecycle and recovery polish
features.description: Complete calibration feedback, HUD, game-over summary, replay, cleanup, and user-facing error states.
features.user_visible_behavior: The player can understand calibration, play a full run, recover from camera errors, and replay without refreshing.
verification: Complete calibration, gameplay, game over, and replay twice without refreshing.
Deny camera access and confirm an actionable error is shown.
Confirm the 35/65 desktop layout and stacked narrow-screen layout remain readable with final styling and reduced-motion preferences enabled.
Run npm run build successfully.
features.notes: Do not add post-MVP exercises or backend features.
